problem: In how many ways can I choose 3 captains from a team of 11 people?

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what it means to choose captains from a team of 11 people?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What do we mean when we say the order of choosing the captains doesn't matter?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose captains when the order does matter? Can you explain?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What does it mean to choose 3 captains out of 11 players? Can you visualize this situation?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Why do we use combinations to solve this problem? What are some characteristics of combinations?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain the formula $\binom{n}{r}$ and how it relates to our problem of choosing 3 captains from 11 players?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you calculate $\binom{11}{3}$? What does this result represent?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any other scenarios or problems where the concept of combinations can be applied?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: What if we wanted to choose more or fewer captains from the team? How would that impact our calculation and the result?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any real-life situations where knowing how to calculate combinations would be useful?
classified_questions.label: e
